[...]
# Sr. Research Scientist, AI Experience (Data Scientist 3)
[...]
This a Full Remote
[...]
offer is available from: California (USA)
[...]
Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health.
[...]
As a Research Scientist, you will be a key contributor to Dexcom’s R&D Engineering Team . ​You will be working with the latest cloud computing platforms and AI technologies to establish an ambitious research program supporting our mission, positioning Dexcom as an industry leader in health AI applications. As a thought leader in your field, you will work closely with other R&D stakeholders to design novel model architectures, develop evaluation pipelines, build concept prototypes, and present your work to peers inside and outside the organization.
[...]
- You help identify long-term research goals as well as intermediate milestones for AI models and applications.
[...]
- You execute research that pushes forward the state of the art in AI domains such as predictive health outcomes, large language models (LLMs), and Generative AI.
[...]
- You directly contribute to experiments training AI models, including experimental design, writing reusable code, running evaluations, and preparing results for broader circulation.
[...]
- You implement new models, tools, and processes used by Dexcom’s core data products team, supporting commercial data product development.
[...]
- Your track record brainstorming new ideas or improving upon existing ideas in machine learning and AI – including designing and executing experiments – demonstrated by accomplishments such as first author publications or projects.
[...]
- You are an adept independent researcher, results-driven, and motivated to solve hard technical challenges including choosing impactful research problems and autonomously carrying out long-running projects.
[...]
- Your experience in creating high-performance implementations of deep learning algorithms, including implementing machine learning and statistical inference algorithms in Pytorch, Tensorflow, etc.
[...]
- You love being on the cutting edge of machine learning and AI research and have a passion for Dexcom’s mission and health sensing applications.
[...]
- You have excellent written and verbal communications skills, and an aptitude for leveraging data visualization software and techniques to tell compelling stories using data.
[...]
- Experience and Education Requirements
[...]
- Typically requires a Bachelor’s degree in a technical discipline, and a minmum of 5-8 years related experience or Master’s degree and 2-5 years equivalent industry experience or a PhD and 0-2 years experience
[...]
- Salary:
[...]
- $116,600.00 - $194,400.00This offer from "Dexcom" has been enriched by Jobgether.com and got a 78% flex score.

results.title: Principal Process Development Engineer - Data Analytics - Dexcom - San Diego, CA, US | Dice.com
highlights:
Principal Process Development Engineer - Data Analytics - Dexcom - San Diego, CA, US | Dice.com
[...]
# Principal Process Development Engineer - Data Analytics
[...]
Machine Learning (ML)
[...]
The Company Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health. We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us.Meet the team: We are seeking a Principal Data Analytics Engineer to serve as a technical authority for advanced data analysis in support of engineering, manufacturing, and quality initiatives across high-volume medical device programs. You will be part of the Global Engineering team within Operations, a team that leads end-to-end technical oversight across external suppliers, contract manufacturers, and specialized automation partners for our wet chemistries, electronics, and hardware systems. You will collaborate closely across R&D, Quality, Regulatory, Supply Chain, and Manufacturing, gaining broad exposure across the full product lifecycle from advance tech development through commercialization. You will partner with programs and project teams to analyze complex data sets, design and interpret Designs of Experiments (DOEs), and drive statistically driven conclusions that inform critical technical and business decisions. Your work will directly support engineers and project teams in the creation of robust protocols and final reports across the whole of the product lifecycle from advanced development, technology transfer, scaling, and on-market support. This includes characterization studies, validations for products and equipment, and root cause investigations, enabling fast, defensible, data-driven paths forward in a highly regulated environment. Where you come in:
[...]
- MS in Statistics, Engineering, Applied Mathematics, Data Science, or related field.
[...]
- Versed in a range of statistical analysis and modeling tools such as control charts, capability analysis, regression, ANOVA, and multivariate methods, measurement system analysis (MSA) response surface models, machine learning, etc. Proficiency with Python, R, SQL, JMP, Minitab, or equivalent tools.
[...]

results.title: Staff Business Intelligence Analyst | Wfhverse
highlights: ) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health. We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us. Meet the team: We are hiring for a Staff Business Intelligence Analyst. In this role, you will be a key contributor to the IT Data and Analytics (ITDNA) Analyst team and the larger Dexcom Analytics Organization, working with ITDNA Data Engineers, to provide actionable insights for business function leaders and internal stakeholders across Dexcom. Along with working to provide key operating metrics and insights, this is the place where you can use your data analysis expertise for good in the world. As a member of our team, you will be leveraging state-of-the-art technologies to create solutions that improve the lives of people with diabetes. Where you come in: Work with stakeholders to collect, analyze, define, and document requirements for data sets, reports, dashboards, metrics, and ad hoc insights Build and maintain effective dashboards, visualizations, reports, and insights using Tableau technologies Participate in Enterprise Analytics Platform (EAP) design and development: define and operationalize new data sets, identify changes to existing data sets Provide end user support/guidance on best practices and approaches to design/implement effective, standardized dashboards and analytics Develop and operationalize efficient BI data models using DBT technologies Analyze new and existing enterprise data sets and develop advanced, multi system analytic models Provide timely execution of assigned tasks in an agile development environment What makes you successful: Proven success functioning in a BI Analyst role Significant experience working with key stakeholders across business functions in finance, marketing, customer support, product management, manufacturing, and supply chain Excellent communication skills both verbally and written Willingness to learn and use different Business Intelligence tools and technologies including but not limited to Tableau, Power BI, and Looker Strong SQL programming skills, including the use of SQL analytic functions 3 or more years generating insights, building dashboards, and developing metrics and KPIs Preferred Skills Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and BigQuery database expertise Moderate Python programming skills What you’ll get: A front row seat to life changing CGM technology. Learn about our brave #dexcomwarriors community. A full and comprehensive benefits program. Growth opportunities on a global scale. Access to career development through in-house learning programs and/or qualified tuition reimbursement. An exciting and innovative, industry-leading organization committed to our employees, customers, and the communities we serve. Travel Required: 0-5% Experience and Education Requirements: Typically requires a Bachelor’s degree in a technical discipline, and

results.title: Sr Manager Biostatistics | Hirevector
highlights: , actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health. We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us. About the Company: Founded in 1999, Dexcom, Inc. provides continuous glucose monitoring technology to help patients and their clinicians better manage diabetes. Since our inception, we have focused on better outcomes for patients, caregivers, and clinicians by delivering solutions for people with diabetes - while empowering our community to take control of diabetes. Meet the team: The Sr Manager Biostatistics will lead a team of biostatisticians supporting all phases of clinical development, from feasibility to pivotal and post-market studies. This role provides strategic statistical guidance, ensures high-quality deliverables, and drives cross-functional collaboration to support regulatory submissions and scientific publications. We have a fast-paced environment and are looking for dynamic people that want to work on an amazing life-changing technology. Where you come in: Lead a team of biostatisticians, managing performance, resource planning, prioritization and timelines. Direct statistical design, analysis, and reporting for feasibility, pivotal, post-market, and RWE clinical studies. Provide strategic guidance on study design, protocol development, CRF review, endpoint selection, statistical analysis methods, and definition of acceptance criteria. Lead or oversee the development and execution of Statistical Analysis Plans (SAPs), ensuring data integrity and analytical rigor. Guide statistical contributions to regulatory submissions (e.g., IDEs, PMAs, 510(k)s) and represent Biostatistics in interactions with regulatory agencies. Support scientific publications through data analysis and interpretation for abstracts, posters, and manuscripts. Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Data Management, Clinical, R&D, Regulatory Affairs, and Medical Affairs. Identify and implement innovative statistical methodologies to address complex study challenges. Ensure adherence to internal standards, regulatory requirements, and industry best practices. Communicate project status, risks, and scope changes to leadership. Provide statistical consultation across departments and perform other duties as assigned. What makes you successful: Experience managing biostatistics teams and operations. Strategic thinker with strong decision-making skills. Proficient in statistical methods and programming; expertise in advanced techniques preferred. Capable of independent work and team leadership with minimal oversight. Strong SAS programming skills; R experience is a plus. Excellent communication and collaboration skills. What you'll get: A front row seat to life changing CGM technology. Learn about our brave #dexcomwarriors community. A full and comprehensive benefits program. Growth opportunities on a global scale. Access to career development through in-house learning programs and/or qualified tuition reimbursement. An exciting and innovative, industry-leading organization committed to our employees, customers, and the communities we serve. Travel Required 0 - 5% Experience and Education M.S. in Biostatistics, Statistics, Data Science, or related field with 10+ years of experience; Ph.D. preferred. Minimum 2 years of team management or

We’re the driving force behind world-class digital experiences that empower millions to manage their health with confidence. What makes us cool? We combine cutting-edge technology, human-centered design, and a collaborative spirit that thrives on creativity and bold ideas. Every day, we shape the future of connected care—crafting intuitive, data-driven tools that truly change lives. If you love tackling big challenges, working with brilliant minds, and seeing your work make a real difference, this is the team you want to grow with.

- Lead the sustained success and evolution of the Dexcom G7 mobile app. You’ll drive product roadmap development, prioritize continuous improvement initiatives, and ensure the app remains clinically trusted, technically robust, and user-friendly across global markets. As a key connector across product, design, engineering, and clinical teams, you’ll help ensure the G7 app remains a trusted, high-performing part of Dexcom’s CGM ecosystem.
[...]
- Serve as the product lead for Dexcom’s G7 and G-series mobile applications—our flagship digital experiences.
[...]
- Own the mobile product roadmap, balancing sustaining work, UX enhancements, and new feature development.

- Drive app reliability, interoperability, and performance optimization based on customer feedback and clinical insights.

[...]
- Drive cross-functional collaboration to define and deliver new, innovative product capabilities.
[...]
- Ensure mobile functionality aligns with CGM sensor performance, meeting system-level reliability and safety standards.
[...]
- Plan for future integrations with insulin delivery systems, partner applications, and Dexcom’s broader digital ecosystem.
[...]
- Use data and analytics to inform prioritization, reduce user friction, improve engagement, and expand global reach.
[...]
- 5–10 years of product management experience, ideally in mobile apps or connected health technology.
[...]
- Deep familiarity with diabetes care technology, CGMs, or medical device ecosystems.
[...]
- Proven success leading product roadmaps and sustaining programs for digital consumer products.
[...]
- Experience gathering and applying voice-of-customer insights to guide product decisions.
[...]
- Cross-functional leadership across engineering, design, clinical, and regulatory teams.
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree in science/engineering, computer science, business, health technology, or related field.
[...]
- Familiarity with agile tools like Jira, Figma, and data analytics platforms.
[...]

[...]
Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health. We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us. Meet the team: The Dexcom Mobile App Product Management team is where innovation meets impact! Every day, we shape the future of connected care—crafting intuitive, data-driven tools that truly change lives. Where you come in : Dexcom is on a mission to empower people with real ‑ time health insights through continuous innovation in sensor technology and connected digital experiences. The Product Manager for Dexcom ONE+ and Flex Mobile Applications will drive the evolution of a growing portfolio of apps designed to meet the unique needs of diverse patient segments across Europe and beyond. These products support differentiated reimbursement constructs, therapy approaches, and user experiences while delivering the same clinically trusted performance and ease of use Dexcom is known for. This role blends hands ‑ on product ownership, cross ‑ functional leadership, and strategic insight into how digital tools can improve lives across varied healthcare systems.

results.title: Sr. Staff Cybersecurity Engineer | Hirevector
highlights: as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health. We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us. Meet the team: Dexcom is a high-growth, fast-paced environment where you work with leading-edge, software teams to ensure the security of modern diabetes medical device systems. You will work alongside highly skilled and passionate innovators who know how to deliver exceptional results while also having some fun. Where you come in: For this Staff Cybersecurity Architect role, you will be joining our Research and Development team to design and review security features for new Dexcom applications. As a member of the R&D Cybersecurity team, you will be responsible for reviewing new mobile applications, identifying, and ranking potential risks, as well as documenting your findings for review. Additionally, you will be responsible for working with the various development teams and product managers to ensure that new projects are implemented correctly and on time. The ideal candidate is one who has previous experience with security-based threat models. You will: Attend design review meetings to identify and document any potential security risks in a new implementation. Document any findings and recommendations for formal review. Oversee implementation of security-based controls to mitigate any potential risks identified during a security review. Advise development team members on security best-practices What makes you successful: 5+ years' experience creating security-based threat models for software applications Experience with a Threat Modeling tool such as Irius Risk and STRIDE Threat Modeling methodology Proven experience working in a mobile APP environment (iOS and/or Android) Experience with using a security-based risk scoring system (CVSS) Knowledge of OWASP top ten vulnerabilities for mobile applications and APIs A solid understanding of symmetric key and public key cryptography and standard security protocols such as TLS and IPsec. Development experience with a common scripting language (e.g., Python, Bash) or programming language (e.g., Swift, Objective C or Kotlin). Ideal if you also have: Previous Mobile Application development and RASP technologies experience Knowledge of industry standards as they relate to mobile APP security management such as OWASP Exposure to agile development, DevOps, SecOps and scrum teams Experience in Medical device industry and HIPAA regulations What you'll get: A front row seat to life changing CGM technology. Learn about our brave #dexcomwarriors community. A full and comprehensive benefits program. Growth opportunities on a global scale. Access to career development through in-house learning programs and/or qualified tuition reimbursement. An exciting and innovative, industry-leading organization committed to our employees, customers, and the communities we serve. Travel Required: 0-5% Experience and Education Requirements: Typically requires a

The average DexCom Data Scientist earns $138,000 annually, which includes a base salary of $107,000 with a $31,000 bonus. This total compensation is $8,495 more than the US average for a Data Scientist.

This position will interface with different cross functional groups (Software Engineering, Software Test, Regulatory Affairs, Marketing, Project managers, Systems Engineering, Cybersecurity, and others). A positive attitude is important while working in a fast-paced environment and dealing with shifting priorities. This is not a test role Where you come in: Actively participates as a core team member of the R&D Quality Assurance Engineering team. Strong understanding of, and ability to provide guidance on IEC 62304, 21 CFR 820, and other relevant regulations worldwide Lead in the effective application, compliance, and continuous improvement of policies, procedures, and practices related to software development and validation across software teams, including: Drive Pre-market risk management deliverables like SHA, design FMEA and support Cyber security risk management Provide guidance to Software Design and test teams, support Software configuration management, tool validations and software integrations. Effectively communicates with the broad Dexcom team and upper-level management on plans, status of tasks, project progress and challenges. Assumes and performs other duties as assigned. Meet the team: Dexcom Clinical Education Team Mission & Overview: Imagine being the voice that turns cutting-edge science into stories that inspire action! As part of Dexcom’s Clinical Education Team, you’ll be the clinical, creative force who transforms breakthrough clinical evidence into powerful, memorable messages. You’ll help healthcare professionals (HCPs), customers, and colleagues see Dexcom CGM not just as a device, but as a game-changer in diabetes care. • Be a Messenger of Innovation: You’ll craft compelling narratives that make complex science accessible and exciting, helping everyone—from healthcare professionals to patients—understand why Dexcom CGM is the future of diabetes care • Make Real Impact: Your work will directly advance awareness and adoption of life-changing technology, improve the lives of people living with diabetes, and shape the standard of care. • Collaborate & Create: Work with passionate teammates, thought leaders, and industry partners to develop clinical programs and content that stands out and makes a difference. • Grow & Shine: Every day brings new opportunities to learn, lead, and leave your mark on the world of diabetes care. If you’re ready to turn evidence into excitement and help drive the next wave of clinical innovation, this is the place for you. Where you come in: • Step into a role where your expertise shapes the future of diabetes care across the nation! As a champion of clinical education, you’ll energize HCPs with the latest in diabetes treatment, medical device training, and CGM innovations. You’ll be one of the go-to leaders for Dexcom Speakers’ Bureau Members and national Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs)—empowering them with the knowledge and tools to support people using CGM technology. • Your influence will directly extend to building and nurturing relationships with KOLs, driving advocacy and education programs, and collaborating to advance CGM adoption. You’ll work hand-in-hand with Marketing, Regulatory, Clinical, Compliance, Customer Support, Field Sales, PR, Medical Affairs, and industry partners—making teamwork and innovation part of your daily routine. • Picture yourself as a vital link between Dexcom’s San Diego Corporate office and field teams nationwide, ensuring everyone from clinicians to consumers has access to the best educational resources. You’ll help craft and deliver key clinical messages to a diverse audience: physicians, NP/PAs, PharmDs, CDCES, and more. • In this role, you’ll be at the forefront of expanding awareness, education, training, and adoption of CGM technology. • If you’re ready to make a nationwide impact, inspire change, and help shape the standard of care for diabetes, Dexcom is ready for you Duties and Responsibilities: KOL Engagement and HCP Educational Content: As a Dexcom Clinical Education leader, you will build powerful partnerships and drive real change in diabetes care. • Be the Connector: Forge and expand relationships with Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs) and influential healthcare professionals nationwide. You’ll be the trusted partner who brings together thought leaders, the Dexcom Speakers Bureau, and our partners to amplify the impact of CGM education. • Expand Your Impact: Work hand-in-hand with internal and industry partners to support the execution of clinical education and market strategy. Your influence will

highlights:
Manager Finance | Jobflarely
[...]
# Manager Finance
[...]
Posted May 29, 2026
[...]
Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health.
[...]
Meet the team:
[...]
Join our high-impact Commercial Finance team in a critical role supporting strategic decision making for our ~$4B U.S. prescription business. As a Commercial Finance Manager, you will translate complex commercial and financial data into actionable insights and range-based forecasts that inform leadership.
[...]
This role offers significant visibility, ownership, and influence. You will work hands-on with large datasets, build and refine forecasting and scenario models, and continuously improve the tools, data, and processes that underpin executive decision making. We are looking for a builder - someone who challenges the status quo, dives into the data, and raises the analytical bar for the team.
[...]
- Serve as a strategic finance partner to Commercial leadership and cross-functional teams including Sales, Marketing, Strategy, Pricing, Supply Chain, and Analytics
[...]
- Own revenue forecasts for current and future product lines, inclusive of five-year projections
[...]
- Translate raw commercial and financial data into clear, concise insights that influence executive-level decisions
[...]
- Present forecast assumptions, scenarios, risks and opportunities with confidence and clarity
[...]
- Build, maintain, and enhance complex forecasting and scenario models, with a deep understanding of underlying drivers and assumptions
[...]
- Own end-to-end analytical workflows—from querying and validating large datasets to modeling outcomes and synthesizing insights; may require writing and/or maintaining queries (e.g. SQL or similar) to extract, join, and analyze data across multiple data sources
[...]
- In partnership with Analytics and Business Intelligence teams, diagnose data quality issues, reconcile inconsistencies, and proactively improve data reliability and analytical outputs
[...]
- Challenge legacy assumptions, tools, and workflows with a first-principles mindset and lead continuous improvement initiatives that enhance forecast accuracy and efficiency
[...]
- Manage and develop team members by setting high analytical standards and coaching both structured problem solving and technical thinking
[...]
- Foster a culture of accountability, learning, and continuous improvement
[...]
What makes you successful:
[...]
Analytical & Technical Capability

- Strong aptitude and track record of quickly learning new tools, systems, and data structures with minimal direction
- Advanced Excel skills with proven experience building robust, auditable financial and scenario models
- Demonstrated ability to independently analyze large, complex datasets and translate findings into business insights
- Hands-on experience querying data (e.g., SQL or similar) and working directly with databases, data warehouses, or analytics platforms
[...]
Business & Leadership Skills
[...]
- Strong financial acumen with experience in forecasting, scenario modeling, and variance analysis
- Ability to operate effectively in ambiguity - framing the right questions, sourcing the right data, and building answers from the ground up
- Proven ability to influence cross-functional partners and gain alignment through data-driven
[...]
- Typically requires a Bachelor’s degree with 8-12 years of industry experience
- 2-5 years of previous management or lead experience

# Medical Science Liaison Manager (Remote)
[...]
Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health.
[...]
We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us.
[...]
Dexcom Clinical Education Team Mission & Overview:
[...]
- Imagine being the voice that turns cutting-edge science into stories that inspire action! As part of Dexcom’s Clinical Education Team, you’ll be the clinical, creative force who transforms breakthrough clinical evidence into powerful, memorable messages. You’ll help healthcare professionals (HCPs), customers, and colleagues see Dexcom CGM not just as a device, but as a game-changer in diabetes care.

KOL Engagement and HCP Educational Content:

Program Development: Amplifying Impact Through Collaboration
[...]
- While your main mission is to engage and empower HCPs and KOLs, you’ll also play a key role in shaping Dexcom’s educational programs and advocacy initiatives. Here’s how you’ll help drive innovation and expand CGM adoption:

Clinical Credentials: You’re a Registered Dietitian (RD), Registered Nurse (RN), Pharmacist (PharmD), Nurse Practitioner, or hold another relevant clinical degree. CDCES certification is required.
[...]
Diabetes Experience: You have at least 5 years of hands-on experience educating and treating people with diabetes, plus 3 years directly working with CGM technology.

Communication Superpowers: Whether you’re talking with partners or HCPs, your written and verbal communication skills are top-notch. You know how to make complex ideas clear and engaging.

Presentation Pro: You’re an expert at developing and presenting PowerPoint presentations that deliver key messages to the right audience. You thrive in front of groups—clinicians, partners, and more—making every session memorable.
[...]
Team Player: Collaboration is in your DNA. You love working with others to achieve shared goals and know how to bring out the best in your teammates.
